name: Mel Lewis
abstract: Melvin Sokoloff (May 10, 1929 – February 2, 1990), known professionally as Mel Lewis, was an American jazz drummer, session musician, professor, and author. He received fourteen Grammy Award nominations.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Newport Jazz Festival: Betty Carter and Her Trio/ Thad Jones-Mel Lewis Orchestra with Dizzy Gillespie, Elvin Jones, and Hank Jones
Newport Jazz Festival: Sarah Vaughan and Her Trio/ Thad Jones-Mel Lewis Orchestra
Kool Jazz Festival: Musicians for Each Other
JVC Jazz Festival: Mel Torme Lonette McKee, Stan Getz, and Dianne Schuur with The Mel Lewis Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: When I actually played with the Thad Jones and Mel Lewis Band... looking at Snooky Young and Jimmy Nottingham, and Mel Lewis...
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: when I played with Thad Jones and Mel Lewis... Mel did an album with me, it’s called “Aerial View”
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: And the rhythm section was Mel Lewis, bass was Bill Crow and the piano was John Bunch.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I met Mel Lewis. One of my Indianapolis friends, Lee Katzman, was on the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: I was in Thad Jones and Mel Lewis‘s band since the first rehearsal.
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Peplowski
about_quotes.evidence: the first one that I ever did the band was Mel Lewis, Milt Hinton, Bucky Pizzarelli, Steve Kuhn, and Buddy Tate was the other saxophone player, and here we are playing somebody’s wedding.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Peplowski interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 9, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]
